blindspots: **IWant revenue impact quantification** — 100% of clients have access but no revenue disclosure. Is it a retention tool (free), a pricing tool (embedded in price increases), or a revenue driver (separate SKU)? This is the single biggest unknown. Monitor via: earnings call language on AI monetization.

**Trust Bank impact on client economics** — Paycom operates a Trust Bank for client funds. Regulatory and interest rate sensitivity. Limited disclosure on economics. Monitor via: 10-K banking subsidiary disclosures.

**Mid-market TAM saturation** — 50-5,000 employee segment. Limited data on total addressable accounts vs penetrated. If saturation is approaching, growth ceiling is lower. Proxy: new client adds trend.

**CapEx trajectory** — $275M capital deployment. No clear breakdown between maintenance and growth. If growth CapEx decelerates, it signals management sees fewer opportunities. Monitor via: 10-K property/equipment disclosures.

cyclical.context: Separates at ~$100M annual. Decline is cyclical, not structural. Will stabilize when rate cycle ends
cyclical.current: Declining as rates fall; masks underlying recurring revenue growth
cyclical.name: Float income

cyclical.context: Recession = fewer employees = lower per-employee rev. BUT payroll is legally mandated = volume floor. Counter-cyclical angle: IWant replaces HR headcount
cyclical.name: Mid-market employment trends

cyclical.context: One-time benefit to margins; don't extrapolate sustained margin expansion from restructuring alone
cyclical.current: Restructuring flowing to margins starting FY2026
cyclical.name: ~540 employee restructuring impact

noiseFilter: **Headline revenue growth rate (6-7%)** — Masks recurring ex-float growing ~10%. Float income decline is cyclical, not structural. Always decompose.
**Chad Richison compensation headlines** — CEO compensation is controversial but has been for years. Not a new thesis risk. $117.5M SBC reversal in FY2024 was one-time and distorted margins.
**Analyst PT revisions** — 22 analysts cover PAYC. Consensus view is well-formed. Our edge is IWant impact assessment, not consensus tracking.
**Single-quarter retention dips** — 91% retention can fluctuate ±1% quarterly. Only sustained 2+ quarter trends matter.
**Share price moves on rate expectations** — Float income sensitivity to rates is known. Stock moves on Fed commentary are mechanical, not fundamental.

structural.current: **+10.3% (FY2025 actual).** Recurring rev $1,938.7M (+10.3%). Total rev $2,051.7M (+9%). Q4: recurring +11.2%. Headline depressed by float income ($103M guided FY2026). 2026 guide: recurring 7-8% (below consensus $2.228B). Stock -7.9% on below-consensus guide.
structural.name: Recurring revenue growth ex-float (YoY)
structural.threshold: <5% for 2+ quarters = structural deceleration confirmed; >10% = re-acceleration thesis intact

structural.current: **91% (FY2025 confirmed).** Improving from 90%. IWant productivity gains translating to stickiness per management. Largest new customer adds since 2022.
structural.name: Client retention rate

structural.current: 100% client base has access. **80% usage growth in Jan 2026** vs Q4 2025 baseline. Forrester study: composite orgs achieve **431% 3-year ROI**, 600 manager hrs/yr saved, 3,600 total employee hrs/yr saved. H3 HR Advisors Signals Award (2026). Globee Award 2025 (AI in HR Mgmt). No revenue quantification yet.
structural.direction: ↑ **Adoption accelerating** — 80% usage growth is first quantitative signal; monetization still unknown
structural.name: IWant AI adoption / traction
structural.source: PAYC Q4 2025 earnings call; Forrester Consulting; H3 HR Advisors
structural.threshold: Measurable revenue contribution disclosed = bull trigger; management declares failed = bear case

structural.current: Management guides conservatively (raised 3x each of last 2 years)
structural.name: Revenue guidance trajectory
structural.threshold: Guidance raise = thesis confirmation; guidance cut = structural concern

structural.current: ~43% adj EBITDA; 95% recurring revenue; zero debt
structural.name: EBITDA margin stability
structural.threshold: <38% = cost structure concern; >45% = operating leverage expanding

trendLog.action: No action — awaiting FY2026 guidance updates
trendLog.assessment: All indicators stable. Headline growth deceptively low (6-7%) due to float decline; recurring ex-float ~10%. IWant has 100% adoption but no monetization data. 22 analysts means no discovery edge — variant must be about magnitude of IWant impact.

trendLog.action: No structural breach. IWant 80% growth is material positive — adoption confirmed, monetization pathway strengthening. Monitor Q1 2026 (~May) for IWant revenue contribution disclosure.
trendLog.assessment: Recurring ex-float ~10% confirmed. Retention 91% improving. EBITDA 43-44% stable. **IWant: 80% usage growth in Jan 2026** — first quantitative adoption signal. Forrester: 431% 3-yr ROI. 2026 guidance below consensus but EBITDA in-line — revenue miss is float-driven.
trendLog.new: Q4: Rev $544.3M (+10.2%), recurring $517.1M (+11.2%), EBITDA 43.4%. FY2025: Rev $2,051.7M (+9%), recurring $1,938.7M (+10.3%), retention 91%. 2026 guide: $2.175-2.195B (below $2.228B consensus), recurring 7-8%, float ~$103M, EBITDA ~44%. Stock -7.9% on below-consensus guide.

flex.name: Data/Network Moat
flex.rationale: Switching costs + platform advantage; client-siloed data, not a network effect; no patents

flex.name: AI Integration Maturity
flex.rationale: IWant: 100% deployed, usage +80%, $100M AI infra, Forrester 431% ROI, retention 90→91%; no AI ARR metric

flex.name: Disruption Misperception
flex.rationale: 22-24 analysts; market fully informed; discount from growth deceleration, not misclassification

flex.name: Recession Armor
flex.rationale: Legally mandated payroll; grew 14% through COVID (filing-confirmed); zero debt; counter-cyclical AI thesis

universal.name: Mispricing Assessment
universal.rationale: 6.6x fwd EBITDA on zero-debt 43%-margin SaaS; 18-63% upside range; FY2024 margin inflated by $117.5M SBC reversal

universal.name: Repricing Mechanism
universal.rationale: Conservative guidance pattern (raised 3x in each of last 2 years), IWant adoption +80%, $1.22B buyback, restructuring → margins

universal.name: Business Quality
universal.rationale: Legally mandated payroll, single-database, 95% recurring, 43% EBITDA, 22% CAGR FY19-24; no patents, gross margin compressing

universal.name: Balance Sheet (GATE)
universal.rationale: Zero debt (filed across 18 quarterly/annual reports), $375M cash, $404M FCF, $1.0B revolver, $1.22B buyback auth
